body:
formal-ai issue link-assistant/formal-ai#492 found that GitHub release notes can show stale or misleading live artifact status badges. The Rust template has the same release-note docs.rs pattern.

Reproducible example:
1. Inspect scripts/create-github-release.rs at current HEAD 63c8595daaa8ef69e61faf783629a60daf6a7266.
2. The GitHub release body prepends: [![Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/{crate_name}/badge.svg)](https://docs.rs/{crate_name}/{normalized_version}).
3. Because docs.rs/{crate}/badge.svg is a live build-status badge, an already-published historical GitHub release can render as docs failing or pending if docs.rs has not built the new version yet or if the docs build later fails.

Workaround:
- Avoid using the live docs.rs badge in release notes. Link to the exact docs.rs version page with text or a static Shields.io badge instead.

Suggested fix:
- Add a helper similar to the existing static crates.io release badge helper, for example: [![Docs.rs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs.rs-{version}-blue)](https://docs.rs/{crate_name}/{version}).
- Add a regression test asserting release-note badges do not contain docs.rs/{crate}/badge.svg but still link to https://docs.rs/{crate}/{version}.
- Consider updating the README docs badge to the Shields docs.rs endpoint as current docs.rs badge guidance points to Shields.io.

This issue is not about removing artifact links; it is about keeping release notes stable so historical releases do not display live failure states.
title: Use static docs.rs artifact badges in GitHub release notes
